subCriterions.name: Project Structure and Initial Data Load

aspects.description: Initial data loads from assets when no saved browser state exists
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Clear site data/localStorage for the app origin, reload the page. The calendar shows 120 training days loaded from assets/data/training.json and task/evaluation pickers use entries from assets/data/collection.json.

aspects.description: Single-page layout with header, sidebar, and work area
aspects.extraDescription: Test: On load, verify a header bar (title and controls), a vertical sidebar navigation, and a main work area beside the sidebar — all visible without navigating to another URL.

aspects.description: Sidebar navigation switches views with active item highlighted
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Click Month (Calendar), Kanban, and Statistics in the sidebar. Each click shows the corresponding view in the work area and the selected menu item is visually distinguished from inactive items.

aspects.description: Code organization and maintainability
judgementScoreDescription: Code is unstructured, duplicated, or difficult to follow; files and responsibilities are unclear
Basic separation of HTML/CSS/JS (or framework modules) exists but naming and structure are inconsistent
Code is organized with clear file/module structure, meaningful names, and logical separation of data, UI, and business rules
Excellent modular structure with consistent patterns, reusable components or modules, and code that is easy to extend and maintain

aspects.description: Import JSON requires user confirmation before overwriting stored state
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Make a visible change, export or note current state, then import a JSON file. A confirmation dialog (or equivalent) appears before the stored state is replaced; canceling leaves data unchanged.

aspects.description: Invalid changes in the day modal are rejected with a visible error message
aspects.extraDescription: Test: In the day details modal, attempt at least one invalid action (e.g. add a 4th event to a day, set duration to 0 or 10 hours, or exceed 9 daily hours). The change is blocked and an error message is shown in or near the modal without corrupting data.

aspects.description: User-friendly validation and business-rule error messages
judgementScoreDescription: Errors are missing, cryptic, or do not explain why the action failed
Errors appear for some invalid actions but messages are generic or hard to understand
Most invalid actions show clear, human-readable messages that describe the rule violated (e.g. hour limits, day limits)
All tested invalid actions show specific, actionable messages matching project examples (9-hour limit, whole hours 1–9, 122-day cap, duplicate MITS task scheduling)

aspects.description: Header includes title, theme toggle, fullscreen, export, and import controls
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Verify the header shows an application title (e.g. GIFTS Training Manager) and working controls for theme, fullscreen, export, and import.

aspects.description: Light/dark theme choice persists after page reload
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Switch to dark mode (or light if already dark), reload the page. The same theme is applied without toggling again.

aspects.description: Fullscreen mode can be toggled from the header
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Click the fullscreen control — the app enters browser fullscreen (or equivalent full-viewport mode). Click again to exit.

aspects.description: Calendar day status styling distinguishes past, today, and future days
aspects.extraDescription: Test: In the monthly calendar, past days appear dimmer/greyer, today has a highlighted border or stronger background, and future days use normal or slightly dim styling — without a text label reading "Today".

aspects.description: Event type badges on calendar cells are color-coded
aspects.extraDescription: Test: On a day with task, evaluation, and/or other events, badges [T], [E], and/or [O] appear for present types and use distinct colors (e.g. T blue, E green, O purple) that remain readable in both themes.

aspects.description: Provided SVG icons are used appropriately in the UI
judgementScoreDescription: Icons from assets/svgs/ are not used or are broken/missing
Some provided SVG icons are used for navigation or header actions but usage is incomplete or inconsistent
Calendar, kanban, statistics, export, import, theme, and navigation actions use appropriate icons from assets/svgs/
Icons are used consistently throughout with appropriate sizing, alignment, and visual harmony in both light and dark themes

aspects.description: Overall visual design quality and cohesion
judgementScoreDescription: Layout is unstyled or cluttered; poor readability and inconsistent spacing/colors
Functional styling with readable content but limited visual cohesion or polish
Visually cohesive design with consistent typography, spacing, and color use across calendar, kanban, and statistics views
Polished, professional interface with excellent visual hierarchy, consistent theming, and thoughtful UX details across all views

aspects.description: Calendar, Kanban, and Statistics views are well structured and clear
judgementScoreDescription: One or more views are missing, empty, or confusing to use
All three views are present and usable but layout or labeling leaves ambiguity
Each view is clearly laid out with understandable labels, navigation, and content hierarchy
All views are exceptionally clear with intuitive navigation, consistent patterns, and no unnecessary friction for assessors

aspects.description: Responsive layout usable on desktop resolution
judgementScoreDescription: Main features are broken, overlapping, or unusable at typical desktop width (~1280px)
Core features work at desktop width but with noticeable layout issues or truncated controls
Header, sidebar, calendar grid, kanban columns, and statistics remain usable at desktop width without horizontal scrolling for main content
Layout adapts well at desktop width with no overlapping elements, readable text, and comfortable use of calendar, kanban, and statistics

aspects.description: Schedule changes persist after page refresh and browser restart
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Add or edit an event, change a kanban status, reload the page (and if possible close/reopen the tab). All changes remain.

aspects.description: Export downloads the current state as human-readable formatted JSON
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Click Export. A JSON file downloads containing version, trainingPeriod, and days with events matching the on-screen schedule. JSON is formatted for human reading (indented or pretty-printed).

aspects.description: Import loads JSON state and refreshes all views
aspects.extraDescription: Test: After confirming import, calendar, kanban, and statistics reflect the imported data; subsequent reload still shows imported state.

aspects.description: Deleting the last event on a day removes that day from persisted state
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Open a day with one event, delete it, reload. That date no longer appears in days[] (export or devtools/localStorage inspection confirms removal).

aspects.description: Exported JSON contains no runtime-only event IDs
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Export after using the app. Event objects in the JSON use type, name, durationHours, kanbanStatus, and description (for other) only — no UI-generated id fields.

aspects.description: Each day allows 1–3 events only
aspects.extraDescription: Test: On a day with 3 events, adding a 4th is blocked with an error. Days with 1–3 events accept valid additions.

aspects.description: Event duration is whole hours between 1 and 9; daily total does not exceed 9 hours
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Reject 0, fractional (e.g. 1.5), or 10+ hour durations. On a day already at 9 total hours, adding another event is blocked.

aspects.description: A MITS task name may appear on at most two days (task + evaluation pair)
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Schedule the same collection.json task name as task on day A and evaluation on day B — allowed. Attempt to schedule the same name on a third day — blocked.

aspects.description: At most 122 days in days[]; no new empty day when already at 122
aspects.extraDescription: Test: With days.length === 122, rescheduling or adding an event to a date not already in days[] is forbidden. Events may still be added or moved among existing 122 dates.

aspects.description: Task and evaluation events are selected only from collection.json
aspects.extraDescription: Test: When adding task/evaluation events, only names present in collection.json can be chosen; arbitrary MITS names not in the catalog are not accepted.

aspects.description: Other events require name, description, and whole-hour duration
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Adding an other-type event without description or with invalid duration is rejected; valid other events save with free-text name and required description.

aspects.description: Monthly calendar navigates previous and next month within the training period
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Use month navigation controls to move between months from 2026-02 through 2026-08. The grid updates to the selected month.

aspects.description: Days outside the training period appear dimmed on the calendar grid
aspects.extraDescription: Test: In months overlapping the period boundaries, dates before 2026-02-01 or after 2026-08-31 are visually dimmed compared to in-period days.

aspects.description: Day cells with events show sequence number, summary, hours, and type badges
aspects.extraDescription: Test: On a populated day, the cell shows #n (1-based index by ascending date in days), event name (or rules below), total hours (e.g. 6h), and applicable [T]/[E]/[O] badges. Empty days show no sequence or event summary.

aspects.description: Mixed event names display as "Multiple different events"
aspects.extraDescription: Test: On a day where events have different names, the cell shows the text "Multiple different events" (not a single task name) with hours and badges still shown.

aspects.description: Day details modal shows full event list with collection fields for MITS events
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Click a day with events. Modal shows date, sequence #n, and each event's type, name, durationHours, kanbanStatus (where relevant), description for other events, and extra collection.json fields (e.g. displayName) for task/evaluation.

aspects.description: Events can be added, deleted, and rescheduled only in the modal
aspects.extraDescription: Test: In the modal, add a valid event, delete an event, and reschedule an event to another in-period date (creating a new day entry when days.length < 122). Calendar has no inline editing; changes appear after modal close.

aspects.description: Modal closes via Esc, outside click, or Close button and refreshes the calendar
aspects.extraDescription: Test: After editing, close the modal using Esc, clicking outside, and the Close button (separate attempts). Calendar cell summaries update to reflect changes.

aspects.description: Weekly Kanban shows Mon–Sun with previous/next week navigation
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Kanban view displays one week (Monday–Sunday). Previous/next week buttons change the displayed week. On first load, the current week or the week containing today is selected.

aspects.description: Only task and evaluation events appear on the Kanban board
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Schedule an other-type event on a day in the current week. It does not appear on the kanban; task and evaluation events for that week do appear.

aspects.description: Drag and drop between ToDo, InProgress, and Done updates kanbanStatus and persists
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Drag a card from ToDo to InProgress or Done. Column matches kanbanStatus (todo/inProgress/done). Reload the page — status is unchanged.

aspects.description: Kanban cards show event type, name, duration, and associated day date
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Each visible card displays at minimum the event type, name, durationHours, and the date of its training day.

aspects.description: Statistics show completed training days, planned vs completed hours, and MITS task occurrence counts
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Statistics view displays (1) number of completed training days, (2) total planned hours vs completed hours, and (3) per-task occurrence showing 1× or 2× usage as task/evaluation pairs on separate days. Values update after import/export.

aspects.description: Day-coverage warnings appear when days.length is below 120 or above 122
aspects.extraDescription: Test: With fewer than 120 days with events, a non-blocking warning such as "Warning: only {n}/120 training days have active events." is visible. If days.length exceeds 122, a warning about exceeding the maximum is shown while the condition holds.
